chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Appl. No. S- 1223 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 11.02.2026 For orders on office objections For hearing of main case Mr. Muhammad Saleem Patoli, Advocate for Applicant Mr. Altaf Hussain Khokhar, A.P.G. Applicant Hamid was one of the people who allegedly accompanied Allah Dino when the alleged offence took place. Allah Dino is the person who allegedly had married Salma and with whom Salma had eloped. Complainant is least interested in these proceedings and has told the police officials that he is no longer interested and that he has moved to Balouchistan. Salma on her own part has also given no objection that Hamid may be released. Be that as it may, an offence under Section 365-B PPC is not compoundable; however, the fact that the complainant and the alleged victim both have lost interest and have both summersaulted on their previous versions for no apparent reason does atleast create doubt in the prosecution case, the benefit of which even at bail stage can go to the applicant. Given the above, the applicant is admitted to post-arrest bail in the sum of Rs. 2,00,000/- (Two Hundred Thousand) and a PR bond for the same amount to the satisfaction of learned trial court. JUDGE karar_hussain/PS*
